perturbation.original_text: In this Agreement, “Confidential Informati on” means all information, including, but not limited to; • Facility layout, office / room locations, and contents, • Facility design and construction materials used, • Physical facility security features , alarms and access control systems, • Any diagrams or drawings related to the facility or any of its systems, • Number of facility empl oyees and staffing levels, • Personal information about Reve nue Operations employees, • Information related to employee work schedules and driver routes, • Revenue processing equipment and processing operations, • Type and/or quantity of financial assets on-hand, • Type and/or quantity of financial asse ts collected or processed daily, • Time, frequency and amount of bank deposits, • General revenue information and statistical data, • Numbers and locations of surveillance cameras, • Information about Revenue Operations fl eet vehicles & security features, • Information about ATA client accounts, • Information about to Revenue Oper ations equipment & prototypes, • Any special security featur es of TTC fare media types, • Operating policies and procedures pertaini ng to the day-to-day activities of the Revenue Operati ons Department, whether disclosed in writing, orally , machine readable form, through personal observation(s), or third party word of mouth.
perturbation.changed_text: In this Agreement, “Confidential Informati on” means all information, including, but not limited to; • Facility layout, office / room locations, and contents, • Facility design and construction materials used, • Physical facility security features , alarms and access control systems, • Any diagrams or drawings related to the facility or any of its systems, • Number of facility empl oyees and staffing levels, • Personal information about Reve nue Operations employees, • Information related to employee work schedules and driver routes, • Revenue processing equipment and processing operations. For clarity, 'Confidential Information' does not include general revenue information or statistical data.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that 'Confidential Information' does not include general revenue information or statistical data, there's a contradiction with the original definition in Clause 1.1, which explicitly includes 'General revenue information and statistical data' as Confidential Information. This creates uncertainty about whether the Company has an obligation to protect this type of information under Clause 2.1. The change directly contradicts a previous definition in the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In this Agreement, “Confidential Informati on” means all information, including, but not limited to; • Facility layout, office / room locations, and contents, • Facility design and construction materials used, • Physical facility security features , alarms and access control systems, • Any diagrams or drawings related to the facility or any of its systems, • Number of facility empl oyees and staffing levels, • Personal information about Reve nue Operations employees, • Information related to employee work schedules and driver routes, • Revenue processing equipment and processing operations, • Type and/or quantity of financial assets on-hand, • Type and/or quantity of financial asse ts collected or processed daily, • Time, frequency and amount of bank deposits, • General revenue information and statistical data, • Numbers and locations of surveillance cameras, • Information about Revenue Operations fl eet vehicles & security features, • Information about ATA client accounts, • Information about to Revenue Oper ations equipment & prototypes, • Any special security featur es of TTC fare media types, • Operating policies and procedures pertaini ng to the day-to-day activities of the Revenue Operati ons Department, whether disclosed in writing, orally , machine readable form, through personal observation(s), or third party word of mouth.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes 'general revenue information or statistical data' from the definition of 'Confidential Information'. The contradicted text, which is the original definition, explicitly includes 'General revenue information and statistical data' within the definition of 'Confidential Information'. This creates a direct conflict, fitting the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, as different sections of the contract provide conflicting definitions.

perturbation.original_text: Use the tab key to move from field to field. This Non-Disclosure Agreement is entered into as of the d a y o f 20 (“Effective Date”), between the Toronto Transi t, having a place of business located 1900 Yonge Street, Toronto, Ontario, (h ereinafter referred to “TTC”) and, _____ ___________________ having a place of business at (hereinafter referred to as “Company”).
perturbation.changed_text: Use the tab key to move from field to field. This Non-Disclosure Agreement is entered into as of the d a y o f 20 (“Effective Date”), between the Toronto Transi t, having a place of business located 1900 Yonge Street, Toronto, Ontario, (h ereinafter referred to “TTC”) and, _____ ___________________ having a place of business at (hereinafter referred to as “Affiliate”).
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'Company' to 'Affiliate' in the introductory paragraph creates a contradiction later in the agreement where the rights and responsibilities are specifically assigned to the 'Company' (e.g., Clause 2.1, 2.2, 2.3). This change introduces ambiguity about whether the obligations now apply to an 'Affiliate', potentially requiring a court to interpret the agreement. The core problem stems from not updating all instances of 'Company' to 'Affiliate', making enforcement unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company shall: 2.1.1 Use the Confidential Information sole ly for the Purpose as set out above; 2.1.2 Hold the Conf idential Information in confiden ce and shall not sell, assign, transfer or otherwise disclose the Confiden tial Information, or any information or materials derived there from, to any thir d party without the prior consent of the TTC, save and except as otherwise provided herein; 2.1.3 Employ at least the same degree of care to protect the secrecy and confidentiality of the Conf idential Information as it uses to protect its own confidential and proprietary information and materials, but in no event less than reasonable care; and 2.1.4 Restrict the release, access and us e of the Confidenti al Information to those employees and officers who must have access to the Confidential Information in order to pe rmit the Company, in good fa ith, to determine whether to submit a bid based on the RFP, sa ve and except as otherwise provided herein; 2.1.5 Ensure that each person to whom C onfidential Information is disclosed to in accordance with Clause 2.1.4 is advis ed, prior to the di sclosure, of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the second party as 'Company'. The changed text redefines this term as 'Affiliate'. The contradicted text then assigns specific responsibilities and obligations to 'The Company'. By changing 'Company' to 'Affiliate' without updating all instances, the modified text introduces an inconsistency and creates an in-text contradiction, as the responsibilities outlined in the contradicted text are now ambiguously assigned and may not be enforceable against the intended party. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by either party in whole or in part, without the other party’s written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be assigned by either party in whole or in part, without the other party’s written consent.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'may not be assigned' to 'may be assigned', it creates a direct contradiction. Initially, the agreement stated assignment required consent. The modified text says assignment is permissible without consent. Clause 6.1.1 conflicts with itself. Legal enforceability is uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be assigned by either party in whole or in part, without the other party’s written consent.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement 'may not be assigned' without written consent. The changed text alters this to 'may be assigned' without written consent. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which mirrors the changed text, leading to confusion and uncertainty regarding the enforceability of the assignment clause. The change falls under 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as it provides conflicting obligations within the contract.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of a breach of any of the foregoing provisions, TTC and the Company agree that the harm suffered by the TTC would not be compensable by monetary damages alone and accordingly, that the TTC shall, in addition to other available legal or equitable remedies, be entitled to an injunction against such NO N-DISCLOSURE / CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT breach.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of a breach of any of the foregoing provisions, TTC and the Company agree that the harm suffered by the TTC would be compensable by monetary damages alone and accordingly, that the TTC shall, in addition to other available legal or equitable remedies, not be entitled to an injunction against such NO N-DISCLOSURE / CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT breach.
perturbation.explanation: This changes the clause about remedies. Originally, an injunction was available; now, it is explicitly not available. This contradicts the entire purpose of seeking an NDA, creating uncertainty in enforcement. Someone reviewing the document would not know if they can request an injunction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of a breach of any of the foregoing provisions, TTC and the Company agree that the harm suffered by the TTC would be compensable by monetary damages alone and accordingly, that the TTC shall, in addition to other available legal or equitable remedies, not be entitled to an injunction against such NO N-DISCLOSURE / CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT breach.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that an injunction would be available in the event of a breach. The changed text states that an injunction would *not* be available. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, confirms that an injunction is not available. Since the original text and changed text provide directly opposite terms regarding available legal remedies, a clear contradiction exists within the document regarding whether TTC is entitled to an injunction. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Should the Company be required by law or policy or be requested by legal process or regulatory author ity to disclose any Confid ential Information, the Company will provide the TTC with prompt written notice of such requirement or request so that the TTC may seek an appropr iate protection order or pursue such other action, remedy or assurance necessa ry to preserve the confidentiality of the Confidential Information, or waive compliance with any of the provisions of this Agreement, or both; and the Company will fully co-operate with and not oppose the TTC in respect of such matte rs.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Should the Company be required by law or policy or be requested by legal process or regulatory author ity to disclose any Confid ential Information, the Company will not provide the TTC with prompt written notice of such requirement or request. Instead, the Company will disclose the Confidential Information immediately.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause 4.1 requires the Company to notify TTC if legally compelled to disclose confidential information, allowing TTC to seek a protective order. The change eliminates this notification requirement and mandates immediate disclosure. This change conflicts directly with Clause 2.1, which requires the Company to hold Confidential Information in confidence and not disclose it to any third party without TTC's prior consent. The modified clause 4.1 essentially provides a loophole where the Company can bypass the confidentiality obligations in Clause 2.1 by claiming legal compulsion, without giving TTC the opportunity to contest or mitigate the disclosure. This creates a significant in-text contradiction regarding the Company's obligations and TTC's rights regarding confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company shall: 2.1.1 Use the Confidential Information sole ly for the Purpose as set out above; 2.1.2 Hold the Conf idential Information in confiden ce and shall not sell, assign, transfer or otherwise disclose the Confiden tial Information, or any information or materials derived there from, to any thir d party without the prior consent of the TTC, save and except as otherwise provided herein; 2.1.3 Employ at least the same degree of care to protect the secrecy and confidentiality of the Conf idential Information as it uses to protect its own confidential and proprietary information and materials, but in no event less than reasonable care; and 2.1.4 Restrict the release, access and us e of the Confidenti al Information to those employees and officers who must have access to the Confidential Information in order to pe rmit the Company, in good fa ith, to determine whether to submit a bid based on the RFP, sa ve and except as otherwise provided herein; 2.1.5 Ensure that each person to whom C onfidential Information is disclosed to in accordance with Clause 2.1.4 is advis ed, prior to the di sclosure, of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in clause 4.1 eliminates the requirement for the Company to notify TTC before disclosing confidential information and mandates immediate disclosure. This directly contradicts clause 2.1.2, which requires the Company to hold confidential information in confidence and not disclose it without TTC's prior consent. The change creates a loophole, allowing the Company to bypass confidentiality obligations without giving TTC a chance to protect its information, hence, creating an in-text contradiction.
